section_miscategorizations.text: Radiographs were three views of the right knee.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is an imaging order/selection rather than a reported result; it belongs in the plan as a diagnostic order, not in the findings section.

section_miscategorizations.text: There is no evidence of loosening or early complications.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is an interpretive diagnostic impression synthesizing imaging/exam, which fits the assessment rather than raw objective findings.

section_miscategorizations.text: Post-operative complications following a right medial unicompartmental arthroplasty, complicated by neuropathic symptoms and altered gait.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The rest of the note's history and chief complaint concern new left hand pain and numbness; this assessment introduces a different problem (right knee arthroplasty complications) not supported by the HPI and would mislead the reader about the visit's diagnostic focus.

section_miscategorizations.text: Continue current medications and monitor for any signs of infection or mechanical failure of the implant.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This plan is directed at managing a right knee implant and its complications, which are not established as active problems in the HPI for left hand pain; it reflects a mismatched problem set and would mislead the reader about the visit's management focus.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's range of motion is 0 to 120 degrees.
section_miscategorizations.reason: As an exam finding it is correctly in Findings, but it appears to refer to the right knee rather than the symptomatic left hand, creating contextual inconsistency with the HPI; organizationally the finding should match the documented chief complaint/problem.

section_miscategorizations.text: Radiographs were three views of the right knee. The patient presented with an antalgic gait. The patient has a healed surgical scar. The patient exhibits a slight limp. Implants are in a stable position. The patient's velocity was decreased.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Individually these are appropriate exam/imaging findings, but collectively they describe a right knee arthroplasty follow-up rather than the left hand complaint documented in the HPI, causing a problem-section mismatch that could confuse the reader about which condition is being evaluated.
